reasoning: The finding is confirmed. In handle_ajax_request() (line 66-77), after a valid nonce check via check_ajax_referer and a current_user_can('edit_posts') gate (Contributor+), raw $_REQUEST is passed directly to prepare_payload($_REQUEST). Inside prepare_payload() (lines 46-53), $_REQUEST['selected_option'] and $_REQUEST['additional_info'] are copied verbatim into the $payload array with no sanitization (no sanitize_text_field, intval, wp_kses, or length restriction). This payload is then passed to send_payload_to_wpdesk() which calls WPDesk_Tracker_Sender_Wordpress_To_WPDesk::send_payload(), which does json_encode($payload) and POSTs the result to https://data.wpdesk.org/?track=1 via wp_remote_post. The full path from attacker-controlled input to external transmission is unobstructed. Auth is correctly identified as Contributor+: the edit_posts capability is the only check. Severity is low because: (1) the WordPress site itself is not impacted - no XSS, SQLi, or stored attack surface within WordPress; (2) the attacker must be authenticated with a valid nonce; (3) the damage is limited to injecting arbitrary data into WPDesk's own third-party telemetry pipeline. No missed sanitizer or guard was found anywhere in the call chain.
